definition: World, especially as the field of experience, beings, and conditioned unfolding spoken of in the teachings.
notes: The project prefers world because it is broad and readable enough for the many contexts in which loka appears. Realm is acceptable when the context points to a domain or sphere more than to the world as a whole, but universe is usually too cosmological for default use.

context_rules.context: In most doctrinal and source-facing contexts
In contexts where the term points more to a domain or sphere of experience

example_phrases.notes: Core doctrinal term with broad contextual range.
